title: When AI goes to war: The dangerous precedent of autonomous weapon systems
description: The realistic fear is incremental normalisation of the use of artificial intelligence in warfare: as lethal discretion shifts from human judgement to autonomous systems, safeguards erode quietly, not through dramatic crises
content: In his 1942 short story Runaround, Isaac Asimov introduced the ‘Three Laws of Robotics’: a robot may not harm a human being; it must obey human orders; and it must protect itself, in that order. Human safety trumped command. Command trumped self-pres... [6625 chars]

title: Arm’s chip adventure is bold bet on AI evolution
description: The $153 bln group’s shares jumped 16% ‌on a plan to sell processors, not just license them. That makes sense as artificial intelligence shifts ​to managing tasks, requiring new chips, ​but brings Arm into a competitive market. ⁠CEO Rene Haas’s $15 bln sales target looks ​challenging.

title: Eli Lilly invests in AI-designed drugs in new deal with Insilico Medicine worth up to €2.39bn
description: Eli Lilly has partnered with AI biotech Insilico Medicine for exclusive global rights to develop and commercialise medicines designed entirely by artificial intelligence.
content:
Eli Lilly and Insilico Medicine have struck a new deal on AI-driven drug discovery, the companies announced on Sunday.

title: Powered Cricket Prediction and Analytics Platform at the Start of IPL 2026
description: New Delhi [India], March 30: Indian cricket has always produced extraordinary numbers. Billions of viewers. Record-breaking audiences. Unmatched commercial value. What it has never had, until now, is a dedicated artificial intelligence platform built specifically to analyse, predict, and explain the game at the level the sport deserves. CricMind.ai changes that -- and it has arrived at precisely the moment it is needed most.

title: Is your job safe in the US? AI could replace millions of careers, survey warns
description: News News: The fear around artificial intelligence isn’t just something you hear at tech summits anymore. It’s showing up in everyday conversations, among coders.
